en: One day at a farmer's market I saw a woman carrying an absolutely gigantic cabbage
When I asked to photograph her she looked really annoyed
And for some reason I was so delighted by her crankiness it seemed so authentic and true Let's
just say what we feel It made me think of all the things women hold literally and metaphorically
And heavy loads and cabbages And stupendous love and courage
And a pink ukulele under a cherry tree And from this a book was formed Women Holding Things
What do women hold The home and the family and the children and the food
The work of the world and the work of being human Sometimes when I'm feeling particularly happy or content I
think I can provide sustenance for legions of human beings
I can hold the entire world in my arms
Other times I can barely cross the room and I drop my arms frozen There is never an
end to holding and certainly there is often the feeling of never doing enough
